content.text: ) are multimodal content types in the Messages API that let you send images (JPEG, PNG, GIF, WebP) and documents (PDF, TXT, DOCX) directly to Claude for analysis. Both use a
object to provide the content, supporting three methods:

(file upload via SDK). Each image/document counts as tokens in your request. Images are used for visual analysis (describe, extract text, identify objects), while documents are used for text extraction, summarization, and content analysis.
Embed the image data directly in the API request as a base64-encoded string.

- Works with any image file
- No external hosting required
- Full control over data

- Increases request size (base64 adds ~33% overhead)
- CPU overhead for encoding large images
Provide a publicly accessible URL to the image instead of embedding it.

(no authentication required)
- Must be a valid HTTP/HTTPS URL
- Claude must be able to fetch the image
- No local file paths (e.g.,

- Smaller request size (no base64 data)
- No encoding overhead
- Good for images already hosted online

- Requires public hosting
- URL must be accessible to Claude
- Slower (Claude must fetch from URL)
Some SDKs support direct binary file upload (no manual encoding).
and may not be available in all versions. Base64 is the most reliable method.
Each image you include in a request counts towards your token usage.
Exact token count depends on image complexity and Claude's internal encoding. Use the
object to see actual token consumption:

message = client.messages.create(...)

print(message.usage)
# Usage(input_tokens=1250, output_tokens=85)
# ^ input includes text + image tokens
Resize large images before sending (e.g., 1024×1024 max for most use cases).
, similar to images. Token count depends on:
Content complexity (text density, images in PDF)
Exact token count depends on Claude's internal encoding. Monitor actual usage:

message = client.messages.create(...)

print(message.usage.input_tokens)
# Includes text + image + document tokens

in a single message. Claude processes them in order.

) are multimodal content types in the Messages API that enable Claude to analyze visual and document content. Key points:
URL method requires publicly accessible HTTP/HTTPS
Images and documents count as tokens (check
Multiple images/documents can appear in same message
Use base64 for most cases (works with any file, no external hosting), and resize/compress large images or documents before sending to reduce token costs.
